method_atom: An information-theoretic capacity-region analysis of a one-to-many communication channel, in which a single source transmits independent messages to multiple receivers; the achievable-rate region is derived as an inner bound from a specific encoding-and-detection scheme, and its tightness is reduced to an extremal optimization conjecture asserting that a distinguished class of input states minimizes an output-entropy functional of the channel.
object_atom: A bosonic (quantum electromagnetic-mode) broadcast channel subject to loss and additive thermal noise, where one transmitter sends to two receivers using coherent-state inputs, characterized via the von Neumann entropy at the channel output.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the capacity-region-via-entropy-minimization-conjecture machinery for a broadcast channel; the domain-specific part is the noisy bosonic quantum channel with coherent-state encoding and von Neumann output entropy.
